title: TopologicalOrder
tex_block_html: Let $G=(J,V,E,L)$ be a CDMG. A topological order of $G$ is a total order $<$ of $J \cup V$ such that for all $v,w \in G$: \[ v \in \Pa^G(w) \; \implies \; v < w.\] Equivalently, it can be described as an indexing of the nodes $J \cup V = \{v_1,\dots,v_K\}$ where parents always precede their children.

tex_statement.raw:
% Definition file for row def_3_8 -- "TopologicalOrder".
% Auto-generated stub by scaffold/solve_chapter.py at row start. The
% manager agent (or a worker dispatched by it) fills the body below with the
% Lean-faithful definition statement(s). Stays close to the lecture notes.
%
% This file is a sibling of `main.tex` inside `<subsection>/tex/`. The
% `subfiles` package lets it compile both standalone (resolving its
% preamble via `main.tex`) and as part of `main.tex` via `\subfile{...}`.

\documentclass[main]{subfiles}

\begin{document}
% ref: def_3_8
% title: TopologicalOrder
\def\rowref{def\_3\_8}
\phantomsection\label{def_3_8}

\begin{Def}[Topological order]
    \label{def-topological-order}
    Let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG (def \ref{def-cdmg}); throughout we use the notation of def \ref{not-cdmg} and the family-relationship notation of def \ref{def_3_5}. By def \ref{def-cdmg}, items~i.--ii., the node set $J \cup V$ is finite (as a union of two finite sets).

    A \emph{topological order} of $G$ is a \emph{strict} total order $<$ on $J \cup V$ -- i.e.\ a binary relation $<$ on $J \cup V$ that is irreflexive, transitive, and satisfies trichotomy ($v < w$, $v = w$, or $w < v$ for every $v, w \in J \cup V$); the lecture notes' order symbol $<$ is read strictly throughout -- such that
    \[
        \forall\, v, w \in J \cup V :\quad v \in \Pa^G(w) \;\Longrightarrow\; v < w.
    \]
    The displayed implication is well-typed because, by the set-builder defining clause $\Pa^G(w) := \lC u \in J \cup V \,|\, (u, w) \in E \rC$ of def \ref{def_3_5}, item~i., any $v \in \Pa^G(w)$ automatically lies in $J \cup V$, so the comparison $v < w$ is defined.

    \emph{Equivalent indexing form.}
    Set $K := |J \cup V|$, the cardinality of the (finite) node set. Because $<$ is a strict total order on the finite set $J \cup V$, its order type is the finite ordinal $K$, so $<$ may equivalently be presented as a bijection $\{1, 2, \dots, K\} \to J \cup V$, $i \mapsto v_i$, with $v_1 < v_2 < \cdots < v_K$ under $<$. Under this presentation, the parents-precede-children condition above becomes
    \[
        \forall\, i, j \in \{1, 2, \dots, K\} :\quad v_i \in \Pa^G(v_j) \;\Longrightarrow\; i < j,
    \]
    i.e.\ \emph{parents always precede their children} in the enumeration $v_1, v_2, \dots, v_K$. The equivalence between the primary (strict-total-order) form and this indexed form relies essentially on the finiteness of $J \cup V$ guaranteed by def \ref{def-cdmg}: without finiteness, the indexed form would be strictly stronger than the primary form (it forces order type at most $\omega$).
\end{Def}

\end{document}

Let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG (def def-cdmg); throughout we use the notation of def not-cdmg and the family-relationship notation of def def_3_5. By def def-cdmg, items i.ŌĆōii., the node set $J \cup V$ is finite (as a union of two finite sets).

A topological order of $G$ is a strict total order $<$ on $J \cup V$ ŌĆō i.e.\ a binary relation $<$ on $J \cup V$ that is irreflexive, transitive, and satisfies trichotomy ($v < w$, $v = w$, or $w < v$ for every $v, w \in J \cup V$); the lecture notes' order symbol $<$ is read strictly throughout ŌĆō such that \[ \forall\, v, w \in J \cup V :\quad v \in \Pa^G(w) \;\Longrightarrow\; v < w. \] The displayed implication is well-typed because, by the set-builder defining clause $\Pa^G(w) := \lC u \in J \cup V \,|\, (u, w) \in E \rC$ of def def_3_5, item i., any $v \in \Pa^G(w)$ automatically lies in $J \cup V$, so the comparison $v < w$ is defined.

Equivalent indexing form. Set $K := |J \cup V|$, the cardinality of the (finite) node set. Because $<$ is a strict total order on the finite set $J \cup V$, its order type is the finite ordinal $K$, so $<$ may equivalently be presented as a bijection $\{1, 2, \dots, K\} \to J \cup V$, $i \mapsto v_i$, with $v_1 < v_2 < \cdots < v_K$ under $<$. Under this presentation, the parents-precede-children condition above becomes \[ \forall\, i, j \in \{1, 2, \dots, K\} :\quad v_i \in \Pa^G(v_j) \;\Longrightarrow\; i < j, \] i.e.\ parents always precede their children in the enumeration $v_1, v_2, \dots, v_K$. The equivalence between the primary (strict-total-order) form and this indexed form relies essentially on the finiteness of $J \cup V$ guaranteed by def def-cdmg: without finiteness, the indexed form would be strictly stronger than the primary form (it forces order type at most $\omega$).

lean_blocks.explanation: This single line is statement-typing infrastructure rather than part of the LN definition itself. The `variable` command auto-binds `Node` (as an implicit `{Node : Type*}`) and the instance `[DecidableEq Node]` into every declaration that follows in this section, so we never have to repeat them on the `IsTopologicalOrder` signature below. The `Type*` annotation is universe-polymorphic ŌĆö `Node` may live at any universe level, matching the convention set in `def_3_1`'s `CDMG`. The `[DecidableEq Node]` instance is load-bearing for this row in two distinct places: first, the `Membership Node (CDMG Node)` instance from `def_3_2` makes `v Ōłł G` reduce to `Finset.mem` on `G.J Ōł¬ G.V`, which needs decidable equality on `Node`; second, `G.Pa w` from `def_3_5` builds its set body around the same `Finset` membership predicate. Drop `DecidableEq Node` and the main statement below stops type-checking.

lean_blocks.code:
def IsTotalOrder (G : CDMG Node) (lt : Node ŌåÆ Node ŌåÆ Prop) : Prop :=
  (ŌłĆ v Ōłł G, ┬¼ lt v v) Ōł¦
  (ŌłĆ u Ōłł G, ŌłĆ v Ōłł G, ŌłĆ w Ōłł G, lt u v ŌåÆ lt v w ŌåÆ lt u w) Ōł¦
  (ŌłĆ v Ōłł G, ŌłĆ w Ōłł G, lt v w Ōł© v = w Ōł© lt w v)
lean_blocks.explanation: This helper carves out the LN's "strict total order on `J Ōł¬ V`" premise as its own named predicate, so `IsTopologicalOrder` below can read literally as "total order *and* parents precede children". The body is a three-way conjunction restricting `lt` to the carrier `J Ōł¬ V`: `ŌłĆ v Ōłł G, ┬¼ lt v v` says `lt` is irreflexive on nodes of `G`; the next conjunct says it is transitive there (a fresh `ŌłĆ u Ōłł G` block per variable, since Lean's bounded `ŌłĆ ŌĆ” Ōłł ŌĆ”` does not chain across multiple binders); the last is trichotomy with equality in the middle slot, following Mathlib's `Trichotomous` disjunct convention. The bounded quantifier `ŌłĆ v Ōłł G, ŌĆ”` desugars to `ŌłĆ v, v Ōłł G ŌåÆ ŌĆ”` and routes membership through the `Membership Node (CDMG Node)` instance from `def_3_2`, which unfolds `v Ōłł G` to `v Ōłł G.J Ōł¬ G.V`. Existing as a separate definition lets `def_3_9`'s `Pred` / `PredLE` take `(h : G.IsTotalOrder lt)` directly without pulling in parent-precedence.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- Named helper carved out of the old flat 4-way Ōł¦ so callers can refer to "total order on J Ōł¬ V" alone.
def IsTotalOrder (G : CDMG Node) (lt : Node ŌåÆ Node ŌåÆ Prop) : Prop :=
  -- (i) irreflexive on the carrier J Ōł¬ V; ŌłĆ v Ōłł G desugars via def_3_2's Membership instance.
  (ŌłĆ v Ōłł G, ┬¼ lt v v) Ōł¦
  -- (ii) transitive on J Ōł¬ V; three separate bounded ŌłĆ's since `ŌłĆ ŌĆ” Ōłł ŌĆ”` does not chain across binders.
  (ŌłĆ u Ōłł G, ŌłĆ v Ōłł G, ŌłĆ w Ōłł G, lt u v ŌåÆ lt v w ŌåÆ lt u w) Ōł¦
  -- (iii) trichotomy on J Ōł¬ V with equality in the middle slot ŌĆö Mathlib's `Trichotomous` convention.
  (ŌłĆ v Ōłł G, ŌłĆ w Ōłł G, lt v w Ōł© v = w Ōł© lt w v)

lean_blocks.code:
def IsTopologicalOrder (G : CDMG Node) (lt : Node ŌåÆ Node ŌåÆ Prop) : Prop :=
  G.IsTotalOrder lt Ōł¦ (ŌłĆ v w, v Ōłł G.Pa w ŌåÆ lt v w)
lean_blocks.explanation: This is the LN's two-tier reading rendered as a nested 2-way `Ōł¦`: a topological order of `G` is a strict total order on `J Ōł¬ V` (the `G.IsTotalOrder lt` half, from the helper above) *and* a parent-precedence clause `ŌłĆ v w, v Ōłł G.Pa w ŌåÆ lt v w` layered on top. Logically equivalent to the older flat 4-conjunct shape ŌĆö `G.IsTotalOrder lt` unfolds definitionally to the same three atomic propositions ŌĆö but the destructure pattern is now nested: `obtain Ō¤©Ō¤©h_irrefl, h_trans, h_totalŌ¤®, h_topoŌ¤® := h` or, more often, `obtain Ō¤©h_to, h_topoŌ¤® := h` followed by further unpacking. `G.Pa w` uses dot-notation through `def_3_5`'s `CDMG.Pa`, matching the LN's $\Pa^G(w)$ notation; no `v Ōłł G` / `w Ōłł G` guards are needed because `Pa`'s body already forces both, and omitting `v ŌēĀ w` is load-bearing so a directed self-loop forces `lt v v`, contradicting irreflexivity. The predicate is `Prop`-valued over an external `lt`, leaving existence for `claim_3_2`'s `G.IsAcyclic Ōåö Ōłā lt, G.IsTopologicalOrder lt` to quantify over.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- Post-refactor 2-way Ōł¦: total-order content + parent-precedence; logically equivalent to the old flat 4-tuple.
def IsTopologicalOrder (G : CDMG Node) (lt : Node ŌåÆ Node ŌåÆ Prop) : Prop :=
  -- LHS reuses the IsTotalOrder helper; RHS is the parent-precedence clause via def_3_5's G.Pa dot-notation.
  G.IsTotalOrder lt Ōł¦ (ŌłĆ v w, v Ōłł G.Pa w ŌåÆ lt v w)

design_choices:
**Typing context `Node : Type*` with `[DecidableEq Node]`, inherited from `def_3_1`.** Both fixtures are load-bearing here. The bounded quantifier `ŌłĆ v Ōłł G, ŌĆ”` driving every conjunct of `IsTotalOrder` routes through the `Membership Node (CDMG Node)` instance from `def_3_2` (`CDMGNotation.lean`), which unfolds `v Ōłł G` to `v Ōłł G.J Ōł¬ G.V` ŌĆö a `Finset` membership check that reduces to decidable equality on `Node`. The parent-precedence clause's `v Ōłł G.Pa w` reaches into `def_3_5`'s parent-set body `{u | u Ōłł G Ōł¦ (u, w) Ōłł G.E}`, whose first conjunct is the same `Finset`-membership predicate and whose second tests pair membership in the `Finset (Node ├Ś Node)` edge set; both routes go through `DecidableEq Node`. Dropping either fixture would make the statement fail to type-check. Stronger instances (`Fintype`, `LinearOrder`) are not needed here and are deferred to consumers ŌĆö `claim_3_2`'s constructive topological-sort proof may need them at the algorithmic step, but they would over-commit this row's interface.

**Total-order content carved out as a named helper `IsTotalOrder`, joined to the parent-precedence clause by a nested 2-way `Ōł¦`.** The LN reads "Let `<` be a *total order* of `J Ōł¬ V` *such that* whenever `v Ōłł Pa^G(w)` we have `v < w`" ŌĆö a two-tier presentation that names "total order on `J Ōł¬ V`" as a substantive sub-concept and then layers parent-precedence on top. The original encoding silently merged the three total-order conjuncts (irreflexive, transitive, trichotomous on `J Ōł¬ V`) into one flat 4-way `Ōł¦` inside `IsTopologicalOrder`, erasing that structure at the type level. The refactor extracts those three conjuncts into `def IsTotalOrder (G) (lt) : Prop := ŌĆ”` (wrapped with three-dash `-- def_3_8 --- start helper` / `end helper` markers, the helper-marker convention used throughout the chapter). All three signals from the helper-predicates checklist are met: *referenced by spec* (the canonical tex literally writes "a *strict* total order `<` on `J Ōł¬ V`"), *substantive content* (three atomic conditions, not a one-liner), and *reused by a downstream row* ŌĆö `def_3_9`'s `Pred` / `PredLE` consume `Pred^G_<(v) = {w Ōłł J Ōł¬ V ŌłŻ w < v}`, meaningful for any total order on `J Ōł¬ V` regardless of parent-precedence, and ch. 5's ID-algorithm and factorisation steps slice or reverse `J Ōł¬ V` via total-order content alone. Without the helper, every such consumer would either re-spell the three atomic conditions or over-import `IsTopologicalOrder`. `IsTopologicalOrder` is then `G.IsTotalOrder lt Ōł¦ (ŌłĆ v w, v Ōłł G.Pa w ŌåÆ lt v w)`.

**The destructure / constructor pattern flips symmetrically from flat 4-tuple to nested 2-tuple.** The two shapes are logically equivalent ŌĆö `G.IsTotalOrder lt` unfolds *definitionally* to the same three atomic propositions in the same order ŌĆö but the pattern-matching syntax changes. The old `obtain Ō¤©h_irrefl, h_trans, h_total, h_topoŌ¤® := h` becomes either `obtain Ō¤©Ō¤©h_irrefl, h_trans, h_totalŌ¤®, h_topoŌ¤® := h` in one step or `obtain Ō¤©h_to, h_topoŌ¤® := h; obtain Ō¤©h_irrefl, h_trans, h_totalŌ¤® := h_to` in two; constructors flip the same way to `refine Ō¤©Ō¤©irrefl_pf, trans_pf, total_pfŌ¤®, topo_pfŌ¤®`. `claim_3_2` (`AcyclicIffTopologicalOrder.lean`) is the test case for both directions ŌĆö forward arm constructs, backward arm destructures ŌĆö and its theorem statement is unchanged; only the proof body's pattern sites move. `def_3_9` benefits as well: it can now take `(h : G.IsTotalOrder lt)` as an explicit hypothesis on `Pred` / `PredLE`, closing the loosened-domain leak where the old shape forced consumers to accept any binary relation or pull in the whole `IsTopologicalOrder` and discard its parent-precedence half.

**`lt : Node ŌåÆ Node ŌåÆ Prop` as an explicit external argument, not a `[LT Node]` typeclass or a bundled structure.** A `[LT Node]` instance would force *exactly one* canonical `<` per `Node` type, but a single `Node` underlies every CDMG in the codebase and each CDMG may admit many topological orders; locking `<` to the type level would make `claim_3_2`'s existence claim either vacuous or unsatisfiable. A typeclass `[CDMG.TopologicalOrder G]` faces the same uniqueness pressure. A bundled `structure TopologicalOrder (G) where lt + props` was rejected on three grounds: it conflates the property (`G.IsTopologicalOrder lt`) with the witness data (`lt`) `claim_3_2` quantifies over, forces callers who just want to *check* a candidate `lt` to package it first, and tempts downstream code to single out "the" canonical order the LN never picks. The same reasoning rules out making `IsTotalOrder` a typeclass: the LN's "total order of `J Ōł¬ V`" is a *property of a chosen relation*, not an instance the type system resolves silently, and a class would force consumers (`def_3_9`'s `Pred`, ch. 4ŌĆō7 factorisation and d-separation, ch. 8ŌĆō10 iSCMs) to either thread `[G.IsTotalOrder lt]` brackets through every signature or rely on the resolver to surface the witness ŌĆö neither matches the LN's pattern of naming `<` explicitly and quantifying over it.

**Domain restricted to `J Ōł¬ V` via `ŌłĆ v Ōłł G, ŌĆ”`, not to the ambient `Node` type via Mathlib's `IsStrictTotalOrder`.** Mathlib's `IsIrrefl`, `IsTrans`, `IsTrichotomous`, and the bundled `IsStrictTotalOrder` all assert their axioms on the *entire* ambient type. The LN's order only needs to relate nodes in `J Ōł¬ V`; using the typeclass would either over-commit `lt` on nodes outside the carrier (a strictly stronger requirement than the LN, ruling out perfectly valid orders that leave non-nodes unrelated) or force a subtype wrapper `Ōåź(G.J Ōł¬ G.V)` that every consumer would have to coerce across. A custom helper `IsStrictTotalOrderOn (S : Finset Node) (lt) : Prop` was considered and rejected as premature scope creep ŌĆö bundling the three conjuncts under yet another fresh name would obscure the LN's plain reading without offering anything `ŌłĆ v Ōłł G, ŌĆ”` does not already. The trichotomy disjunct order `lt v w Ōł© v = w Ōł© lt w v` (equality in the middle) follows Mathlib's `Trichotomous` convention, easing any later lift to a subtype `Trichotomous` instance.

**`G.Pa w` via dot-notation through `def_3_5`'s namespaced parent operator, no `v Ōłł G`/`w Ōłł G` guard, no `v ŌēĀ w` precondition.** The parent clause spells the relation as `G.Pa w` rather than `Pa G w`: `def_3_5` defines `Pa` inside `namespace CDMG`, so `G.Pa w` reads naturally as "the parents of `w` in `G`" through Lean's dot-notation, matching the LN's $\Pa^G(w)$ shorthand letter-for-letter. Spelling the implication directly as `(v, w) Ōłł G.E ŌåÆ lt v w` would lose the `v Ōłł G` witness baked into `Pa`'s body. The universal `ŌłĆ v w, v Ōłł G.Pa w ŌåÆ lt v w` needs no bounded prefix because `def_3_5`'s `Pa` body already forces `v Ōłł G` and `def_3_1`'s edge-endpoint subset clause forces `w Ōłł G` whenever `(v, w) Ōłł G.E`. Omitting `v ŌēĀ w` is load-bearing: when `(v, v) Ōłł G.E` (a directed self-loop), the implication forces `lt v v`, which contradicts irreflexivity (the first conjunct of `IsTotalOrder`). The mere existence of any topological order therefore rules out directed self-loops on `J Ōł¬ V` ŌĆö exactly what `claim_3_2`'s `ŌćÉ` direction needs, and the reason a `v ŌēĀ w` guard would silently break it.

**Scope choice: primary strict-total-order form only; the indexed reformulation is a downstream theorem.** The canonical tex spells out both the strict-total-order form and the equivalent "`J Ōł¬ V = {v_1, ŌĆ”, v_K}` with `v_1 < ŌĆ” < v_K`" indexing, and proves them equivalent under finiteness ŌĆö any strict total order on a finite set has order type a finite ordinal and so can be presented as an enumeration. We encode only the primary form: finiteness of `J Ōł¬ V` is available *when needed* via `def_3_1`'s `Finset`-valued `J, V`, so any consumer can derive the indexed form on demand (via `Finset.toList` ordered by `lt`, or a `Fin K ŌåÆ Node` bijection) without polluting this row's API. A parallel `IsTopologicalOrderIndexed` predicate would invite spurious case-splits at every downstream site over which form is in scope, while the LN treats the two as logically identical presentations of one notion. No Mathlib re-use beyond what `IsTotalOrder` itself uses (`Membership Node (CDMG Node)` from `def_3_2`, `Set` membership on `G.Pa w` from `def_3_5`); no `IsStrictTotalOrder` / `LinearOrder` / `Preorder` for the domain-restriction reasons above.
